scope: custom: combat -- R9 phase-dependent reach + speed tempo (armature reset 2026-05-29)
note: The spear fix. Phase-dependent reach + speed tempo: reach governs the closing/breaking measure (longer weapon controls approach -- canonical 'reach = -1 Ob closing'), speed governs the bind (faster weapon wins once closed; reach INVERTS to a liability -- HEMA measure/bind dynamic). Self-test 5/5. Grounded bottom-up (canonical reach + per-weapon speed, combat_v30 §5 + m3) AND top-down (HEMA). Gap-completion: canon says 'spear is king' but the sim left it dead (the gap between canonical intent and mechanical realization); does NOT overwrite an authored value. FINDING (honest): R9 gives the spear/dagger their correct phase niches (self-tested), but landing all 8 weapons strictly in-band is a JOINT-FIT problem -- reach helps every LONG weapon (spear AND war hammer), so the war hammer stays high (76-79%) even with the armour taper AND a hypothetical x3->x2 multiplier taper; the two 'long-weapon advantages' (reach-at-distance + heavy damage/survivability) compound and no single knob separates them. Full convergence needs a joint fit or a design call (e.g. reach benefits light-long but not heavy-long weapons), flagged for Jordan. R9 is the validated MECHANISM; the magnitudes are sim-tunable. Class A = canonical reach/speed (combat_v30 §5; m3). Class C = phase-tempo magnitudes (grounded; sim-tunable). PROPOSAL -- Jordan-vetoable; no canon file edited. [V2: reach-CONTROL now scales with LIGHTNESS (light long = full, heavy long = 0.25) + speed is an all-phase term -> a heavy slow weapon (war hammer) is disadvantaged in EVERY duel phase; its damage edge only pays off in the battlefield context (R10). Confirmed: war hammer duel dominance is DAMAGE not tempo (fells in 3 strikes vs blades' 4-6); no tempo channel offsets a 2x damage gap.]

entries.quoted_text: Reach: long weapon imposes -1 Ob on the opponent during closing (reach = -1 Ob closing). Expressed as a sigma-leverage edge to the longer weapon in the closing/breaking phases; magnitude 0.8*LEVEL_SIGMA[minor]. Bottom-up: canonical reach rule. Top-down: HEMA -- reach dominates the approach.

In the bind, the faster weapon wins the close exchange; dsig per canonical speed-point gap = 0.45*LEVEL_SIGMA[minor]. Bottom-up: canonical per-weapon speed. Top-down: HEMA -- the faster/shorter weapon dominates the grapple.
Minor speed edge in the closing phase (quickness helps a little even at range); 0.15*LEVEL_SIGMA[minor]. Smaller than the bind speed term -- speed matters most once closed.

Reach inverts to a liability in the bind: the long weapon cannot bring its point to bear inside the measure; penalty 0.5*LEVEL_SIGMA[minor] per reach-step. Top-down: HEMA -- a spearman who lets you close past the point is in trouble. The canonical rule names reach as a closing advantage; the bind-inversion is the grounded gap-fill.

Heavy long weapons retain only 0.25 of full reach-CONTROL: they have the reach but commit to each swing and cannot fence at distance. Bottom-up: canonical weight axis. Top-down: HEMA -- a light spear out-fences a heavy hammer at the same length.
